methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $144M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Atomic Machines is a Series A chip design company founded by Jeff Holden and Prashant Patil. The valuation analysis draws on two applicable methods across a comp universe of 4 private-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) plus 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the company's $144M total raised, yielding $288M. The Peer-Median method anchors to the four same-stage, same-sector private comparables, which span $500M to $5.0B with a median of $1.5B. Public comp anchoring via P/S multiples (22.3x median across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies. These two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $245M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $894M.

The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty around Atomic Machines' technology maturity and go-to-market trajectory relative to peers. The peer set exhibits substantial heterogeneity—a 10x spread from Rain AI to Etched—suggesting stage-adjusted positioning and differentiation matter materially. Without disclosed revenue or customer traction, the Stage-Progression floor ($288M) offers a conservative anchor, while the Peer-Median ceiling ($1.5B) reflects potential at the upper end of the comp range.
